I am going to scout the vault first, then narrow to the most philosophically interesting concepts.

```python
ROOT = "__ROOT__"
print("=== Sampling files to understand corpus shape ===")
print(sample_files(8))
```

The filenames alone are not enough, so I should also inspect content-level signals.

```
print("=== Searching for philosophical and identity concepts ===")
hits = grep_open("existential|identity|introspection|belief|meaning", limit=6, window=1)
for hit in hits[:3]:
    print(hit)
```

Now I will read the strongest files directly.

```python
aimlessness = read_file(f"{ROOT}/Aimlessness.md", limit=60)
sexual = read_file(f"{ROOT}/Sexuality/Sexual Urges Are Elusive to Introspection.md", limit=60)
belief = read_file(f"{ROOT}/Belief.md", limit=60)
print("=== Loaded key files ===")
print(aimlessness[:300])
print(sexual[:300])
print(belief[:300])
```

I will analyze the strongest candidates in parallel.

```python
import asyncio

instruction = "Extract the most philosophically novel concept in 1 sentence."

async def analyze():
    return await asyncio.gather(
        async_llm_query(aimlessness[:1200], instruction),
        async_llm_query(sexual[:1200], instruction),
        async_llm_query(belief[:1200], instruction),
    )

analyses = asyncio.run(analyze())
for item in analyses:
    print(item)
```

```python
FINAL("\n".join(analyses))
```
